<<<KOT:mythologisation>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"mythologisation is a morphological derivative (nominalization of the verb mythologize/mythologise, itself derived from the adjective mythological). Its sense depends on understanding the productive morphosyntactic operation of converting a verb to a gerund or abstract noun. The base verb — to construct or treat something as mythology — is itself a higher-order cognitive act (making something into a story/belief system) that requires metalinguistic reference to narrative, belief, and cultural practice. This exceeds what the prime lexicon can express without accessing culture-specific or abstract philosophical concepts like 'narrative', 'culture', or 'belief-system' that are not atomic primes and not in the catalog."}
<<<END:mythologisation>>>>

<<<KOT:neuroglia>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"neuroglia are specialized cells defined by their role in the central and peripheral nervous systems and by their histological/functional properties (non-electrical-impulse-producing cells that support neurons, form myelin, maintain homeostasis, etc.). The everyday grounding would require reference to neurons, myelin, homeostasis, and specific anatomical subsystems (central nervous system, peripheral nervous system) whose identity conditions are technical-biological and not expressible as molecules or explication-level primes. The concept belongs to the biology/anatomy sector and requires domain-specific grounding not available in the kernel catalog."}
<<<END:neuroglia>>>>

{"kind":"explication","gloss":"this something is not joined with another thing into one thing; these two things are apart.","record":{"schema":"kot-ast/1","frame":"WhenTrue","referents":[{"index":1,"refKind":"SomethingRef"},{"index":2,"refKind":"SomethingRef"}],"clauses":[{"type":"op","op":"NOT","args":[{"type":"pred","pred":"BE-SPEC","roles":{"undergoer":{"kind":"ref","index":1},"attribute":{"kind":"sp","head":{"kind":"partFrame","of":{"kind":"ref","index":2}}}}}]},{"type":"pred","pred":"BE-SPEC","roles":{"undergoer":{"kind":"ref","index":1},"attribute":{"kind":"sp","mods":[{"mod":"OTHER~ELSE~ANOTHER"}],"head":{"kind":"primeHead","prime":"SOMETHING~THING"}}}}]}}
<<<END:nonintegrated>>>>

<<<KOT:nylghau>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"nylghau (also nilghai) is a proper biological name for a specific animal species (Boselaphus tragocamelus, a large Asian antelope). It is a taxon-specific label, not an everyday concept with stable semantic content independent of zoological classification. While 'animal' is in the catalog, this species has no defining everyday perceptual or behavioral features that distinguish it from other large animals in the prime lexicon (unlike 'dog', 'bird', or 'rabbit', which have recognized everyday affordances and bodily traits). The concept requires external biological/geographic reference to ground."}
<<<END:nylghau>>>>
